url.description: Base URL of the Grafana instance

tempoUid.description: UID of the Tempo data source in Grafana

lokiUid.description: UID of the Loki data source in Grafana

ttlSeconds.description: Time-to-live for sessions in seconds. If not set, uses global SESSION_TTL.

cleanupMode.description: Cleanup mode: 'full' deletes everything, 'anonymize' keeps metadata but removes PII.

capacity.description: The capacity of the status list. If not set, uses global STATUS_CAPACITY.

bits.description: Bits per status entry: 1 (valid/revoked), 2 (with suspended), 4/8 (extended). If not set, uses global STATUS_BITS.

ttl.description: TTL in seconds for the status list JWT. If not set, uses global STATUS_TTL.

immediateUpdate.description: If true, regenerate JWT immediately on status changes. If false (default), use lazy regeneration on TTL expiry.

enableAggregation.description: If true, include aggregation_uri in status list JWTs for pre-fetching support (default: true).

sessionConfig.description: Session storage configuration for this tenant. Controls TTL and cleanup behavior.

statusListConfig.description: Status list configuration for this tenant. Only affects newly created status lists.

id.description: The unique identifier for the tenant.

status.description: The current status of the tenant.

clients.description: The clients associated with the tenant.

allowedPresentationConfigs.description: List of presentation config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

allowedIssuanceConfigs.description: List of issuance config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

clientId.description: The unique identifier for the client.

secret.description: The secret key for the client.

tenantId.description: The unique identifier for the tenant that the client belongs to. Only null for accounts that manage tenants, that do not belong to a client

roles.description: The roles assigned to the client.

tenant.description: The tenant that the client belongs to.

statusListConfig.description: Status list configuration for this tenant. Only affects newly created status lists.

id.description: The unique identifier for the tenant.

sessionConfig.description: Session storage configuration. Controls TTL and cleanup behavior.

statusListConfig.description: Status list configuration for this tenant. Only affects newly created status lists.

sessionConfig.description: Session storage configuration. Controls TTL and cleanup behavior.

allowedPresentationConfigs.description: List of presentation config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

allowedIssuanceConfigs.description: List of issuance config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

roles.description: The roles assigned to the client.

allowedPresentationConfigs.description: List of presentation config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

allowedIssuanceConfigs.description: List of issuance config IDs this client can use. If empty/null, all configs are allowed.

clientId.description: The unique identifier for the client.

secret.description: The secret key for the client.

roles.description: The roles assigned to the client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the status list

credentialConfigurationId.description: Credential configuration ID to bind this list exclusively to. Leave empty for a shared list.

keyChainId.description: Key chain ID to use for signing. Leave empty to use the tenant's default StatusList key chain.

capacity.description: Capacity of the status list. If not provided, uses tenant or global defaults.

bits.description: Bits per status value. If not provided, uses tenant or global defaults.

status_lists.description: Array of status list token URIs

capacity.description: The capacity of the status list. Set to null to reset to global default.

bits.description: Bits per status entry. Set to null to reset to global default.

ttl.description: TTL in seconds for the status list JWT. Set to null to reset to global default.

immediateUpdate.description: If true, regenerate JWT on every status change. Set to null to reset to default (false).

enableAggregation.description: If true, include aggregation_uri in status list JWTs for pre-fetching support. Set to null to reset to default (true).

id.description: Unique identifier for the status list

credentialConfigurationId.description: Credential configuration ID this list is bound to. Null means shared.

keyChainId.description: Key chain ID used for signing. Null means using the tenant's default.

capacity.description: Total capacity of the status list

uri.description: The public URI for this status list

expiresAt.description: JWT expiration timestamp. Null if JWT has not been generated yet.

credentialConfigurationId.description: Credential configuration ID to bind this list exclusively to. Leave empty for a shared list.

keyChainId.description: Key chain ID to use for signing. Leave empty to use the tenant's default StatusList key chain.

bits.description: Bits per status value. More bits allow more status states. Defaults to tenant configuration.

capacity.description: Maximum number of credential status entries. Defaults to tenant configuration.

credentialConfigurationId.description: Credential configuration ID to bind this list exclusively to. Set to null to make this a shared list.

keyChainId.description: Key chain ID to use for signing. Set to null to use the tenant's default StatusList key chain.

authorization_details.description:
RFC 9396 authorization details. When passed via
application/x-www-form-urlencoded (PAR) the value is a JSON string; when
passed inside a signed request object it can already be an array.

response_type.description: The type of response expected for the offer request.

credentialClaims.description: Credential claims configuration per credential. Keys must match credentialConfigurationIds.

flow.description: The flow type for the offer request.

tx_code.description: Transaction code for pre-authorized code flow.

tx_code_description.description: Description for the transaction code (e.g., "Please enter the PIN sent to your email").

credentialConfigurationIds.description: List of credential configuration ids to be included in the offer.

authorization_server.description: Optional authorization server to be used for this issuance flow.

webhookEndpointId.description: ID of the webhook endpoint to notify about the status of the issuance process.

type.description: The type of authentication used for the webhook.

headerName.description: The name of the header where the API key will be sent.

value.description: The value of the API key to be sent in the header.

type.description: The type of authentication used for the webhook.

config.description:
Configuration for API key authentication.
This is required if the type is 'apiKey'.

auth.description:
Optional authentication configuration for the webhook.
If not provided, no authentication will be used.

includeRawTokensFor.description: List of credential IDs to include raw tokens for (e.g., ['sca_credential'])

url.description: The URL to which the webhook will send notifications.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the request was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the request was last updated.

expiresAt.description: The timestamp when the request is set to expire.

useDcApi.description: Flag indicating whether to use the DC API for the presentation request.

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this object.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token for the session - used to obtain a new access token.

refresh_token_expires_at.description:
Expiration timestamp for the refresh token.
Used to validate refresh_token grant requests.

request_uri.description: Request URI from the authorization request.

auth_queries.description:
Authorization queries associated with the session.
Encrypted at rest.

offer.description:
Credential offer object containing details about the credential offer or presentation request.
Encrypted at rest.

offerUrl.description: Offer URL for the credential offer.

credentialPayload.description:
Credential payload containing the offer request details.
Encrypted at rest - may contain sensitive claim data.

webhookEndpointId.description: ID of the webhook endpoint to notify about issuance status.

requestUrl.description: The URL of the presentation auth request.

credentials.description:
Verified credentials from the presentation process.
Encrypted at rest - contains personal information.

vp_nonce.description: Noncce from the Verifiable Presentation request.

clientId.description: Client ID used in the OID4VP authorization request.

walletNonce.description:
Cryptographic random nonce used in wallet-facing URLs (response_uri, request_uri, state).
Per OID4VP spec Section 13.3, this separates the wallet-facing identifier (request-id)
from the frontend-facing session ID (transaction-id) to prevent session fixation.

responseCode.description:
Cryptographic random code generated after successful VP Token processing.
Per OID4VP spec Section 13.3, included in redirect_uri so only the legitimate
frontend (which receives the redirect) can confirm the session completed.

responseUri.description: Response URI used in the OID4VP authorization request.

redirectUri.description: Redirect URI to which the user-agent should be redirected after the presentation is completed.

parsedWebhook.description: Where to send the claims webhook response.

transaction_data.description:
Transaction data to include in the OID4VP authorization request.
Can be overridden per-request from the presentation configuration.

externalSubject.description:
The subject (sub) from the external authorization server token.
Used to identify the user at the external AS.

errorReason.description:
Error reason if the session failed.
Stores the error message when status is 'failed'.

sessionId.description: The session ID of the user

credentialConfigurationId.description:
The ID of the credential configuration
This is optional, if not provided, all credentials will be revoked of the session.

status.description:
The status of the credential
0 = valid, 1 = revoked, 2 = suspended

ttlSeconds.description: Time-to-live for sessions in seconds. Set to null to use global default.

cleanupMode.description: Cleanup mode: 'full' deletes everything, 'anonymize' keeps metadata but removes PII.

temporaryPassword.description: One-time temporary password returned only on user creation.
One-time temporary password returned only on user creation.
One-time temporary password returned only on user creation.

url.description:
The URL used in the OID4VCI authorized code flow.
This URL is where users will be redirected for authentication.

webhook.description: Optional webhook configuration for authentication callbacks

type.description: Link to the presentation configuration that is relevant for the issuance process

issuer.description: The OIDC issuer URL of the upstream provider

clientId.description: The client ID registered with the upstream provider

clientSecret.description: The client secret for confidential clients

scopes.description: Scopes to request from the upstream provider

signingKeyId.description: Key ID for signing access tokens. If unset, the default signing key is used.

chainedAs.description:
Configuration for Chained Authorization Server mode.
When enabled, EUDIPLO acts as an OAuth AS facade, delegating user authentication
to an upstream OIDC provider while issuing its own tokens with issuer_state.

refreshTokenEnabled.description: Whether refresh tokens should be issued for OID4VCI token responses.

credentialResponseEncryption.description: Whether `credential_response_encryption` should be advertised in the credential issuer metadata.

refreshTokenExpiresInSeconds.description: Refresh token lifetime in seconds. Defaults to 2592000 (30 days).

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this object.

authServers.description: Authentication server URL for the issuance process.

batchSize.description:
Value to determine the amount of credentials that are issued in a batch.
Default is 1.

dPopRequired.description: Indicates whether DPoP is required for the issuance process. Default value is true.

walletAttestationRequired.description:
Indicates whether wallet attestation is required for the token endpoint.
When enabled, wallets must provide OAuth-Client-Attestation headers.
Default value is false.

walletProviderTrustLists.description:
URLs of trust lists containing trusted wallet providers.
The wallet attestation's X.509 certificate will be validated against these trust lists.
If empty and walletAttestationRequired is true, all wallet providers are rejected.

preferredAuthServer.description:
The URL of the preferred authorization server for wallet-initiated flows.
When set, this AS is placed first in the `authorization_servers` array
of the credential issuer metadata, signaling wallets to use it by default.
Must match one of the configured auth servers, the chained AS URL, or "built-in".

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the VP request was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the VP request was last updated.

signingKeyId.description: Key ID for signing access tokens. If unset, the default signing key is used.

chainedAs.description:
Configuration for Chained Authorization Server mode.
When enabled, EUDIPLO acts as an OAuth AS facade, delegating user authentication
to an upstream OIDC provider while issuing its own tokens with issuer_state.

refreshTokenEnabled.description: Whether refresh tokens should be issued for OID4VCI token responses.

credentialResponseEncryption.description: Whether `credential_response_encryption` should be advertised in the credential issuer metadata.

refreshTokenExpiresInSeconds.description: Refresh token lifetime in seconds. Defaults to 2592000 (30 days).

authServers.description: Authentication server URL for the issuance process.

batchSize.description:
Value to determine the amount of credentials that are issued in a batch.
Default is 1.

dPopRequired.description: Indicates whether DPoP is required for the issuance process. Default value is true.

walletAttestationRequired.description:
Indicates whether wallet attestation is required for the token endpoint.
When enabled, wallets must provide OAuth-Client-Attestation headers.
Default value is false.

walletProviderTrustLists.description:
URLs of trust lists containing trusted wallet providers.
The wallet attestation's X.509 certificate will be validated against these trust lists.
If empty and walletAttestationRequired is true, all wallet providers are rejected.

preferredAuthServer.description:
The URL of the preferred authorization server for wallet-initiated flows.
When set, this AS is placed first in the `authorization_servers` array
of the credential issuer metadata, signaling wallets to use it by default.
Must match one of the configured auth servers, the chained AS URL, or "built-in".

label.description: Optional label for this step (for display purposes)

presentationConfigId.description: ID of the presentation configuration to use for this step

label.description: Optional label for this step (for display purposes)

url.description: URL to redirect the user to for web-based interaction

callbackUrl.description: URL where the external service should redirect back after completion. If not provided, the service must call back to the IAE endpoint.

description.description: Description of what the user should do on the web page (for wallet display)
description.example: Please complete the identity verification form

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook endpoint

key_storage.description: List of required key storage types (e.g., iso_18045_high, iso_18045_moderate)

user_authentication.description: List of required user authentication types (e.g., iso_18045_high, iso_18045_moderate)

path.description: Path to the claim. For SD-JWT: JSONPath-like array. For mDOC: [namespace, claim_name]

mandatory.description: Whether this claim must be disclosed

display.description: Display information for the claim in different locales

keyAttestationsRequired.description:
Key attestation requirements for JWT proofs for this credential.
When set, this is published in proof_types_supported.jwt.key_attestations_required
for this specific credential configuration.

docType.description:
Document type for mDOC credentials (e.g., "org.iso.18013.5.1.mDL").
Only applicable when format is "mso_mdoc".

namespace.description:
Namespace for mDOC credentials (e.g., "org.iso.18013.5.1").
Only applicable when format is "mso_mdoc".
Used when claims are provided as a flat object.

claimsMetadata.description:
Claims metadata for wallet rendering.
Follows the OID4VCI credential_metadata.claims specification.
Each claim includes a path (JSONPath-like array), optional mandatory flag,
and display information with multi-language support.

Example:
[
  { "path": ["given_name"], "mandatory": false, "display": [{ "name": "Given Name", "locale": "en-US" }] },
  { "path": ["address", "street_address"], "display": [{ "name": "Street Address", "locale": "en-US" }] }
]

id.description:
Unique identifier for the key chain.
This is the ID referenced by other entities (e.g., issuance config's signingKeyId).

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for the key chain.

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this key chain.

description.description: Human-readable description of the key chain.

usageType.description: The purpose/role of this key chain in the system.

usage.description: The usage type of the keys (sign or encrypt).

kmsProvider.description:
The KMS provider used for this key chain.
References a configured KMS provider name.

externalKeyId.description:
External key identifier for cloud KMS providers.
This field stores the provider-specific key reference for the active signing key.

rootCertificate.description:
Root CA certificate in PEM format.
Self-signed certificate for the root CA key.

activeCertificate.description:
Certificate for the active signing key in PEM format.
Either CA-signed (if rootKey exists) or self-signed.

rotationIntervalDays.description: Rotation interval in days. Key material will be rotated after this many days.

certValidityDays.description: Certificate validity in days when generating new certificates.

lastRotatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the key was last rotated.

previousCertificate.description: Certificate for the previous signing key in PEM format.

previousKeyExpiry.description:
Expiry date for the previous key.
After this date, the previous key should be deleted.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the key chain was last updated.

vct.description: VCT as a URI string (e.g., urn:eudi:pid:de:1) or as an object for EUDIPLO-hosted VCT

iaeActions.description: List of IAE actions to execute before credential issuance

embeddedDisclosurePolicy.description:
Embedded disclosure policy (discriminated union by `policy`).
The discriminator makes class-transformer instantiate the right subclass,
and then class-validator runs that subclass’s rules.

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this object.

attributeProviderId.description:
Reference to the attribute provider used for fetching claims.
Optional: if set, claims will be fetched from this provider during issuance.

webhookEndpointId.description:
Reference to the webhook endpoint used for notifications.
Optional: if set, notifications will be sent to this endpoint.

keyChainId.description:
Reference to the key chain used for signing.
Optional: if not specified, the default attestation key chain will be used.

iaeActions.description: List of IAE actions to execute before credential issuance

The discriminator makes class-transformer instantiate the right subclass,
and then class-validator runs that subclass’s rules.

Optional: if set, claims will be fetched from this provider during issuance.

Reference to the key chain used for signing.
Optional: if not specified, the default attestation key chain will be used.

The discriminator makes class-transformer instantiate the right subclass,
and then class-validator runs that subclass’s rules.

Reference to the attribute provider used for fetching claims.
Optional: if set, claims will be fetched from this provider during issuance.

Reference to the webhook endpoint used for notifications.
Optional: if set, notifications will be sent to this endpoint.

keyChainId.description:
Reference to the key chain used for signing.
Optional: if not specified, the default attestation key chain will be used.

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook endpoint
Unique identifier for the webhook endpoint

Optional registrar-side certificate identifier.
If provided and still valid, EUDIPLO reuses it instead of creating a new certificate.

body.description:
Registration certificate creation payload.
This is merged with tenant-level registrar defaults when a certificate is created.

jwt.description:
Optional pre-existing registration certificate JWT.
If provided, EUDIPLO forwards it as-is and does not create a new one.

registrationCertCache.description: Server-managed cache of the materialized registration certificate. Read-only; values supplied by clients are ignored.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VP request.

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this object.

lifeTime.description: Lifetime how long the presentation request is valid after creation, in seconds.

dcql_query.description: The DCQL query to be used for the VP request.

registrationCert.description: The registration certificate request containing the necessary details.

webhook.description: Optional webhook URL to receive the response.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the VP request was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the VP request was last updated.

redirectUri.description:
Redirect URI to which the user-agent should be redirected after the presentation is completed.
You can use the `{sessionId}` placeholder in the URI, which will be replaced with the actual session ID.

accessKeyChainId.description:
Optional ID of the access certificate to use for signing the presentation request.
If not provided, the default access certificate for the tenant will be used.

Note: This is intentionally NOT a TypeORM relationship because CertEntity uses
a composite primary key (id + tenantId), and SQLite cannot create foreign keys
that reference only part of a composite primary key. The relationship is handled
at the application level in the service layer.

issuerUrl.description: Issuer URL or full OpenID4VCI metadata URL to resolve server-side.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VP request.

lifeTime.description: Lifetime how long the presentation request is valid after creation, in seconds.

dcql_query.description: The DCQL query to be used for the VP request.

registrationCert.description: The registration certificate request containing the necessary details.

webhook.description: Optional webhook URL to receive the response.

Redirect URI to which the user-agent should be redirected after the presentation is completed.
You can use the `{sessionId}` placeholder in the URI, which will be replaced with the actual session ID.

Optional ID of the access certificate to use for signing the presentation request.
If not provided, the default access certificate for the tenant will be used.

Note: This is intentionally NOT a TypeORM relationship because CertEntity uses
a composite primary key (id + tenantId), and SQLite cannot create foreign keys
that reference only part of a composite primary key. The relationship is handled
at the application level in the service layer.

lifeTime.description: Lifetime how long the presentation request is valid after creation, in seconds.

dcql_query.description: The DCQL query to be used for the VP request.

registrationCert.description: The registration certificate request containing the necessary details.

Redirect URI to which the user-agent should be redirected after the presentation is completed.
You can use the `{sessionId}` placeholder in the URI, which will be replaced with the actual session ID.

Note: This is intentionally NOT a TypeORM relationship because CertEntity uses

privacy_policy.description: Default privacy policy URL for registration certificate creation.

support_uri.description: Default support contact URI for registration certificate creation.

provided_attestations.description: Default provided attestations list for registration certificate creation.

registrarUrl.description: The base URL of the registrar API

oidcUrl.description: The OIDC issuer URL for authentication (e.g., Keycloak realm URL)

clientId.description: The OIDC client ID for the registrar

clientSecret.description: The OIDC client secret (optional, for confidential clients)

registrationCertificateDefaults.description: Optional default values merged into registration certificate creation requests (for example privacy_policy, support_uri, provided_attestations)

hasPassword.description: Indicates whether a password is configured (actual password is never returned)

registrarUrl.description: The base URL of the registrar API

oidcUrl.description: The OIDC issuer URL for authentication (e.g., Keycloak realm URL)

clientId.description: The OIDC client ID for the registrar

clientSecret.description: The OIDC client secret (optional, for confidential clients)

password.description: The password for OIDC login (stored in plaintext)

registrationCertificateDefaults.description: Optional default values merged into registration certificate creation requests (for example privacy_policy, support_uri, provided_attestations)

registrarUrl.description: The base URL of the registrar API

oidcUrl.description: The OIDC issuer URL for authentication (e.g., Keycloak realm URL)

clientId.description: The OIDC client ID for the registrar

clientSecret.description: The OIDC client secret (optional, for confidential clients)

password.description: The password for OIDC login (stored in plaintext)

registrationCertificateDefaults.description: Optional default values merged into registration certificate creation requests (for example privacy_policy, support_uri, provided_attestations)

keyId.description: The ID of the key to create an access certificate for

transaction_id.description: The transaction identifier previously returned by the Credential Endpoint

request_uri.description: The request URI for the Pushed Authorization Request.

expires_in.description: The expiration time for the request URI in seconds.

interaction_types_supported.description: Comma-separated list of supported interaction types (for initial request)

request_uri.description: The request URI to use at the authorization endpoint

expires_in.description: The lifetime of the request URI in seconds

code.description: Authorization code received in the callback (authorization_code grant)

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI (must match the one used in PAR)

refresh_token.description: Refresh token (issued when refresh tokens are enabled)

crossDeviceUri.description: URI for cross-device flows (no redirect after completion)

claims.description: Claims to include in the credential. The structure should match the credential configuration's expected claims.

status.description: The new status of the transaction

error.description: Optional error message explaining why the issuance failed

kty.description: The key type, which is always 'EC' for Elliptic Curve keys.

crv.description: The algorithm intended for use with the key, such as 'ES256'.

x.description: The x coordinate of the EC public key.

y.description: The y coordinate of the EC public key.

keys.description: An array of EC public keys in JWK format.

response.description:
The response string containing the authorization details (JWE-encrypted VP token).
Required for success responses, absent for error responses.

sendResponse.description: When set to true, the authorization response will be sent to the client.

error_uri.description: URI with additional information about the error.

state.description: State value from the authorization request (for correlation).

data.description: The full trust list JSON (generated LoTE structure)

id.description: Unique identifier for the trust list

tenantId.description: The tenant ID for which the VP request is made.

tenant.description: The tenant that owns this object.

data.description: The full trust list JSON (generated LoTE structure)

entityConfig.description:
The original entity configuration used to create this trust list.
Stored for round-tripping when editing.

sequenceNumber.description: The sequence number for versioning (incremented on updates)

jwt.description: The signed JWT representation of this trust list

sequenceNumber.description: The sequence number at the time this version was created

data.description: The full trust list JSON at this version

entityConfig.description: The entity configuration at this version

jwt.description: The signed JWT at this version

canImport.description: Whether the provider supports importing existing keys.

canCreate.description: Whether the provider supports generating new keys.

canDelete.description: Whether the provider supports deleting keys.

name.description: Unique provider ID (matches the id in kms.json).

type.description: Type of the KMS provider (db, vault, aws-kms).

description.description: Human-readable description of this provider instance.

providers.description: Detailed info for each registered KMS provider.

enabled.description: Whether automatic key rotation is enabled.

id.description: Unique identifier for the key chain.

usageType.description: Usage type of the key chain.

type.description: Type of key chain (standalone or internalChain).

kmsProvider.description: KMS provider used for this key chain.

rootCertificate.description: Root CA certificate (only for internalChain type).

activePublicKey.description: Active signing key's public key info.

activeCertificate.description: Active signing key's certificate. Not present for encryption keys.

previousPublicKey.description: Previous signing key's public key info (if in grace period).

previousCertificate.description: Previous signing key's certificate (if in grace period).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the key chain was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the key chain was last updated.

usageType.description: Usage type for this key chain.

key.description: The private key in JWK format (EC).

crt.description: Certificate chain in PEM format (leaf first, then intermediates/CA).

enabled.description: Whether automatic key rotation is enabled.

intervalDays.description: Rotation interval in days. Required when enabled is true.

certValidityDays.description: Certificate validity in days. Defaults to rotation interval + 30 days grace period.

usageType.description: Usage type determines the purpose of this key chain (access, attestation, etc.).

type.description: Type of key chain to create.

description.description: Human-readable description for the key chain.

kmsProvider.description: KMS provider to use (defaults to the configured default provider).

rotationPolicy.description: Rotation policy configuration. Only applicable for the signing key (root CA never rotates).

enabled.description: Whether rotation is enabled. When true, the imported key becomes a root CA.

id.description: ID for the key chain. If not provided, a new UUID will be generated.

key.description: The private key in JWK format.

usageType.description: Usage type for this key chain.

crt.description: Certificate chain in PEM format (leaf first, then intermediates/CA).

kmsProvider.description: KMS provider to use. Defaults to 'db'.

rotationPolicy.description: Rotation policy. When enabled, the imported key becomes a root CA and a new leaf key is generated.

enabled.description: Whether automatic key rotation is enabled.

description.description: Human-readable description for the key chain.

activeCertificate.description: Active certificate chain in PEM format. Used for external certificate updates.

response_type.description: The type of response expected from the presentation request.

webhook.description:
Webhook configuration to receive the response.
If not provided, the configured webhook from the configuration will be used.

redirectUri.description:
Optional redirect URI to which the user-agent should be redirected after the presentation is completed.
You can use the `{sessionId}` placeholder in the URI, which will be replaced with the actual session ID.

transaction_data.description:
Optional transaction data to include in the OID4VP request.
If provided, this will override the transaction_data from the presentation configuration.
